name: Visibility Module - AWS 2.x Upgrade Plan
description: Create a detailed AWS 2.x upgrade plan for the visibility module. Produce visibility-aws2x-plan.md and CONFLUENCE-visibility.md under visibility/docs. Reference patterns from webbl, booking-bridge, and booking modules.

entries.summary: Session started: Reading current state doc and reference materials from webbl, booking-bridge, and booking modules
entries.detail: Read DESIGN-curr-state.md (visibility), DESIGN.md (webbl, booking-bridge), CONFLUENCE-webbl.md. Now reading remaining Confluence reference docs and will checkout booking feature branch for its design docs.

entries.summary: Analysis complete: 11 sub-modules, 134 test files, 5 DAOs, 4+ DynamoDB entity classes, heavy AWS SDK v1 usage
entries.detail:
Comprehensive analysis completed:
- 11 sub-modules (6 DW services, 4 Lambdas, 1 shared lib)
- 134 test files total
- 5 DAO classes extending DynamoDBCrudRepository (dynamo-client)
- 4 DynamoDB entity classes with @DynamoDBTable annotations: ContainerEvent, ContainerEventOutbound, ContainerEventPending, ContainerTrackingEvent, BookingDetailVisibility
- AWS SDK v1 used for: DynamoDB (DynamoDBMapper), SQS (AmazonSQS), S3 (AmazonS3), SNS (SNSClient wrapper), SSM (AWSSimpleSystemsManagement), Lambda events
- SES v2 already used in visibility-inbound
- commons version: 1.R.01.021, dynamo-client version: 1.R.01.021
- Booking dependency: com.inttra.mercury:booking:2.1.8.M (needs upgrade to cloud-sdk version)
- Reference modules read: webbl DESIGN.md + CONFLUENCE-webbl.md, booking-bridge DESIGN.md + CONFLUENCE_DESIGN_DOC.md, booking DESIGN-AWS2x.md + CONFLUENCE_DESIGN_DOC.md

entries.summary: Both documents created successfully: visibility-aws2x-plan.md and CONFLUENCE-visibility.md
entries.detail: Created both deliverables:\n1. visibility/docs/visibility-aws2x-plan.md — Comprehensive upgrade plan with 19 sections covering all 11 sub-modules, POM changes, DynamoDB model migration, SQS/S3/SNS/SES/SSM migration, unit test plan, integration test plan, verification strategy, risk matrix, file change matrix, and tooling commands used.\n2. visibility/docs/CONFLUENCE-visibility.md — Confluence design document following the pattern from webbl, booking-bridge, and booking modules with all standard sections (Requirements, HLD, LLD, Configuration, Security, Test Plan, etc.)

entries.summary: Documented key architectural decisions for visibility AWS SDK 2.x upgrade
entries.detail:
Key architectural decisions for the visibility AWS upgrade:
1. Phase approach: commons first (Phase 2), then 6 DW services (Phase 3), then 4 Lambdas (Phase 4), then booking dep (Phase 5)
2. SQS: Keep custom SqsMessageHandler polling framework but swap internals to cloud-sdk MessagingClient<String>; change processor interface from Message to QueueMessage<String>
3. DynamoDB: All 5 DAOs move from DynamoDBCrudRepository to DatabaseRepository wrapper pattern; ~9 new AttributeConverters for complex nested types (JSON-serialized)
4. S3 Archiver Lambda: Replace DynamoDB Document API with DynamoRepositoryFactory (no Guice in Lambda)
5. SES: Migrate inbound's direct SesClient to cloud-sdk EmailService via VisibilityEmailModule
6. SSM: Lambda functions migrate from SDK v1 AWSSimpleSystemsManagement to SDK v2 SsmClient or cloud-sdk paramstore API
7. Booking dependency upgrade deferred to Phase 5 (requires booking module merge first)
8. New Guice modules: VisibilityDynamoModule, VisibilityMessagingModule, VisibilityStorageModule (shared across all DW services)
9. Integration tests: Use BaseDynamoDbIT from dynamo-integration-test for ~30-40 new DynamoDB integration tests

entries.summary: Session complete - all deliverables produced. Model: Claude Opus 4.6
entries.detail:
Model: Claude Opus 4.6

- CONFLUENCE-visibility.md: Standard Confluence design doc following webbl/booking-bridge/booking pattern with all required sections

entries.summary: Fixed section numbering in visibility-aws2x-plan.md after branch strategy insertion
entries.detail: After inserting Section 2 (Branch & Git Strategy) via multi_replace_string_in_file, section numbers were inconsistent (duplicate ## 4. and ## 9.). Applied 34 replacements to renumber all sections and subsections to be consecutive 1-20. TOC was already correct from the initial insertion. All 20 section headings now verified consecutive.

entries.summary: CORRECTED: Booking commit only touches booking/ files (223). Zero overlap with develop. Cherry-pick is conflict-free.
entries.detail:
Git analysis of booking commit 74f36e7a71 vs latest develop:

1. Booking commit touches ONLY 223 booking/ files — zero visibility, zero db-migration, zero network files. Previous analysis was incorrect (compared full branch diff, not single commit).

2. Develop is 11 commits ahead of booking branch base (abee9cc33d):
   - ION-15057: touches 5 visibility files (CargoVisibilityEventProcessor, CargoVisibilitySubscriptionProcessor + tests, visibility/pom.xml)
   - ION-14875: Commercial Entity Role changes
   - One develop commit (43a5f917ff ION-15057) touches booking/ files but different ones than the booking AWS commit

3. ZERO file overlap between booking commit and new develop commits — cherry-pick onto latest develop is guaranteed conflict-free.

4. Booking branch does NOT need rebasing — the commit itself only touches booking/ and there's no overlap.

Conclusion: Cherry-pick strategy is even cleaner than originally thought. No visibility files in the booking commit means the rebase-drop is trivially clean.

entries.detail:
Updated both documents:

1. visibility-aws2x-plan.md:

   - Fixed 'Why This Works' section: removed incorrect '5 visibility files' reference, added 'self-contained' explanation
   - Updated Edge Case section: clarified draft PR will show booking + visibility separately

2. CONFLUENCE-visibility.md:

   - Updated Open Issues: booking dep version now says 'compiled against cherry-picked commit'
   - Added new 'Branch Strategy' subsection under Assumptions with link to plan doc Section 2

entries.summary: FINAL: All documents reviewed, corrected, and verified. Plan subsections renumbered, Phase 5 corrected, Confluence doc self-contained with inline branch strategy. Session context updated with full implementation details.
entries.detail:
FINAL REVIEW — All documents and session context updated. Summary of all corrections applied:

## Document Fixes Applied
1. Plan doc subsection renumbering: Section 6 (Phase 2) subs 5.x→6.x + 5.1.x→6.1.x, Section 7 (Phase 3) subs 6.x→7.x, Section 8 (Phase 4) subs 7.x→8.x
2. Plan doc Phase 5 (Section 9): Removed incorrect claim that booking commit changes visibility files. Corrected to state booking commit only provides upgraded booking/ code (BookingDetail, booking DAOs, Guice modules, converters). Removed action item 4 about reviewing '5 visibility files'
3. Confluence doc: Removed plan.md cross-reference. Inlined full branch strategy with all key facts and step-by-step workflow
4. Previous fixes: TOC + section heading numbering 1-20, Verified Facts correction (223 booking-only files), Why This Works rewrite

## Git Branching Strategy (verified 2026-03-30)
- Branch name: feature/visibility-aws-sdk-2x-upgrade
- Base: latest develop (HEAD = ab1f48b483)
- Cherry-pick: 74f36e7a71 from feature/ION-14382-bk3-aws-upgrade-2
- Booking commit scope: 223 files, ALL in booking/ — zero overlap with develop HEAD
- develop is 11 commits ahead of booking branch base (abee9cc33d)
- New develop commits touching visibility: ION-15057 (5 files: CargoVisibilityEventProcessor, CargoVisibilitySubscriptionProcessor + tests, visibility/pom.xml)
- Rebase-drop: When booking merges, git rebase -i develop → drop booking cherry-pick → force-with-lease
- Edge case: If PR ready before booking merge, draft PR or change base to booking branch

## Implementation Phases (from plan)
- Phase 0: Branch setup + cherry-pick
- Phase 1: POM changes (add cloud-sdk 1.0.22-SNAPSHOT, remove dynamo-client)
- Phase 2: visibility-commons (DynamoDB models, 9 AttributeConverters, 5 DAOs, SQS/S3/SNS wrappers, 3 Guice modules, config)
- Phase 3: 6 Dropwizard services (Guice module updates, processor QueueMessage<String> migration)
- Phase 4: 4 Lambda functions (factory-based client creation, SSM migration)
- Phase 5: Booking dependency alignment (BookingDetailVisibility, BookingDao, BookingServiceImpl)
- Verification: mvn verify -pl visibility -am

## Key Technical Details
- Commons remains at 1.R.01.021, cloud-sdk at 1.0.22-SNAPSHOT
- 5 DynamoDB entity classes, 5 DAO classes, 7 SQS processors, 9 new AttributeConverters
- 134 existing tests to migrate + ~30 new unit tests + ~30-40 new DynamoDB integration tests
- Integration tests use BaseDynamoDbIT from dynamo-integration-test (DynamoDB Local, no Docker)
- Reference modules: webbl (merged), booking-bridge (merged), booking (feature branch)

## Deliverables
- visibility/docs/visibility-aws2x-plan.md — 20 sections, fully numbered and consistent
- visibility/docs/CONFLUENCE-visibility.md — Self-contained, no external references, ready for Confluence upload
- Model: Claude Opus 4.6
